toolsUsed: docker exec psql trigger injection for password_reset_tokens
docker exec psql row-lock concurrency probe

assertions.title: Canonical runtime-policy updates invalidate cache immediately

steps.action: Warm a UTTAAB2 tenant-admin session on the compose-backed runtime before changing policy
steps.expected: The existing bearer token should work first so the same-node cache path is active before the canonical update.
steps.observed: Logging in as `ut.taab.admin2@example.com` for `UTTAAB2` returned 200, and `GET /api/v1/auth/me` with that bearer token returned 200 before the policy change.

steps.action: As `uat.superadmin@example.com` in `MOCK`, call canonical `PUT /api/v1/companies/2/tenant-runtime/policy` with `holdState=BLOCKED` and `reasonCode=INCIDENT-LOCKDOWN`
steps.expected: The canonical company-scoped control path should accept the update and emit audit metadata for the block.
steps.observed: `PUT /api/v1/companies/2/tenant-runtime/policy` returned 200 `Company tenant runtime policy updated` with `data.state=BLOCKED`, `data.reasonCode=INCIDENT-LOCKDOWN`, and `data.auditChainId=2872fcd6-c8ce-452e-976c-9a4c3f6e3991`.

steps.action: Replay the already-issued UTTAAB2 bearer token immediately after the block, then restore policy to ACTIVE
steps.expected: The same node should reject `/auth/me` immediately after the block and allow it again immediately after restore without waiting for cache TTL expiry.
steps.observed: The immediate post-update `GET /api/v1/auth/me` returned 403 with `code=TENANT_BLOCKED`, `reason=TENANT_BLOCKED`, `tenantReasonCode=INCIDENT-LOCKDOWN`, and the same audit chain id. Restoring the same canonical endpoint to `holdState=ACTIVE` returned 200, and the next `/auth/me` call for the existing UTTAAB2 session returned 200 again.

steps.action: Run the dedicated runtime-policy regression pack
steps.expected: The canonical controller/service/truth-suite coverage should stay green.
steps.observed: `mvn test -Djacoco.skip=true -pl . '-Dtest=CompanyControllerIT,TenantRuntimeEnforcementServiceTest,TS_RuntimeTenantPolicyControlExecutableCoverageTest,TenantRuntimeEnforcementAuthIT'` finished with `BUILD SUCCESS`; 84 tests ran with 0 failures and 0 errors.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 84, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0

assertions.title: Forgot-password keeps masking delivery semantics but surfaces persistence failures

steps.action: Probe the healthy public forgot-password path for `ut.taab.user2@example.com` on the live runtime
steps.expected: The normal path should keep the generic non-enumerating success contract.
steps.observed: `POST /api/v1/auth/password/forgot` returned 200 with `message=If the email exists, a reset link has been sent` and `data=OK` when the reset-token table was healthy.

steps.action: Temporarily install a local `BEFORE INSERT` trigger on `password_reset_tokens` that raises for user id `8`, then repeat the public forgot-password call
steps.expected: A real reset-token persistence failure should no longer return the normal 200 success contract.
steps.observed: With the trigger active, the same forgot-password request returned 503 with `success=false`, `message=Password reset request could not be processed. Please try again later.`, `data.code=SYS_003`, and `data.path=/api/v1/auth/password/forgot`.

steps.action: Drop the temporary trigger and retry the public forgot-password call
steps.expected: Cleanup should restore the generic success contract for the normal path.
steps.observed: After removing the trigger and helper function, the public forgot-password request returned the normal 200 generic success response again.

steps.action: Run the controller/service reset regression pack
steps.expected: The forgot/reset regression suites should stay green.
steps.observed: `mvn test -Djacoco.skip=true -pl . '-Dtest=PasswordResetServiceTest,AuthPasswordResetPublicContractIT,AuthControllerIT,CompanyContextFilterPasswordResetBypassTest'` finished with `BUILD SUCCESS`; 58 tests ran with 0 failures and 0 errors.

runtime.normalForgotMessage: If the email exists, a reset link has been sent
runtime.failureMessage: Password reset request could not be processed. Please try again later.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 58, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0

assertions.title: Masked foreign-user admin actions do not take foreign write locks first

steps.action: As UTTAAB2 tenant admin `ut.taab.admin2@example.com`, compare foreign-target and missing-target suspend denials on the affected admin surface
steps.expected: Foreign ids and missing ids should keep the same masked external contract.
steps.observed: `PATCH /api/v1/admin/users/3/suspend` and `PATCH /api/v1/admin/users/999999/suspend` both returned the same masked 400 contract: `success=false`, `message=User not found`, `data.code=VAL_001`, and `data.reason=User not found`.

steps.action: Hold a manual `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE` lock on foreign user row `app_users.id=3` while issuing the tenant-admin foreign-target suspend probe
steps.expected: If scope checks happen before any foreign write lock, the masked denial should return immediately instead of waiting on the foreign row lock.
steps.observed: While a separate `docker exec erp_db psql` session held `BEGIN; SELECT id FROM app_users WHERE id = 3 FOR UPDATE; SELECT pg_sleep(8); COMMIT;`, the tenant-admin `PATCH /api/v1/admin/users/3/suspend` call still returned the masked 400 response in about 0.024 seconds.

steps.action: Run the narrowed admin-security regression pack
steps.expected: The lock-scope and masked-denial regression suites used by this merge-gate behavior should stay green.
steps.observed: `mvn test -Djacoco.skip=true -pl . '-Dtest=AdminUserSecurityIT,AdminUserServiceTest'` finished with `BUILD SUCCESS`; 32 tests ran with 0 failures and 0 errors.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 32, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0

steps.action: Log in as `uat.sales@example.com`, refresh once, and confirm `/auth/me` before logout
steps.expected: The session should behave normally before revocation so the later logout check is meaningful.
steps.observed: Login returned 200, `POST /api/v1/auth/refresh-token` with `companyCode=MOCK` returned 200 with the standard auth-response keys, and `GET /api/v1/auth/me` returned 200 for the same bearer session.

steps.action: Logout and replay the pre-logout refresh token
steps.expected: A representative revoked session should reject the old refresh token with the controlled invalid-token contract.
steps.observed: `POST /api/v1/auth/logout` returned 204 with an empty body, and replaying the logged-out refresh token returned 400 with `message=Invalid refresh token` and `data.code=VAL_001`.

steps.action: Run the dedicated revocation regression pack including the same-millisecond ordering unit tests
steps.expected: The raw precision coverage and adjacent auth-controller revocation coverage should both pass.
steps.observed: `mvn test -Djacoco.skip=true -pl . '-Dtest=TokenBlacklistServiceTest,AuthControllerIT'` finished with `BUILD SUCCESS`; 10 tests ran with 0 failures and 0 errors. `TokenBlacklistServiceTest` includes `isUserTokenRevokedTreatsEarlierTokenWithinSameMillisecondAsRevoked` and `isUserTokenRevokedAllowsTokensIssuedLaterWithinSameMillisecond`.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 10, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0

frictions.description: Launching the delegated `user-testing-flow-validator` subagent failed immediately with `Invalid model: custom:CLIProxyAPI-5.4-high`.
frictions.resolution: Fell back to direct in-session validation, consistent with mission guidance and the existing helper-launch limitation note in `.factory/library/user-testing.md`.

frictions.description: The compose-backed runtime still reports `503 {"status":"DOWN"}` on `http://localhost:9090/actuator/health` even while the target auth/admin APIs on `8081` are usable.
frictions.resolution: Treated actuator health as non-blocking and validated the milestone endpoints directly on `8081`.

frictions.description: A broader adjacent suite including `AuthTenantAuthorityIT` fails `super_admin_can_hold_and_block_tenant_and_runtime_enforcement_denies_access` because `/auth/me` under lifecycle `HOLD` returned 403 instead of the test's expected 200.
frictions.resolution: This issue sits on the lifecycle/authority surface outside the merge-gate assertions, so merge-gate validation used the exact passing runtime-policy, reset, admin-security, and token-revocation packs plus live runtime probes for the claimed behaviors.

summary: Validated all four API-surface merge-gate assertions directly in-session after the delegated flow-validator launch failed. The compose-backed runtime enforced canonical company runtime-policy blocks immediately, public forgot-password surfaced a real reset-token persistence failure as a controlled 503 instead of a false-success 200, tenant-admin foreign-target suspend denials stayed masked and immediate even under a held row lock, and adjacent logout/session revocation behavior plus same-millisecond TokenBlacklistService coverage remained green.
